rationale: The query's core transferable method is a mean-field dynamical model of spreading (infections/distress) on scale-free networks with a vanishing epidemic threshold due to heavy-tailed degree distributions. The candidate studies rumor spreading using mean-field equations on complex networks, but its focus is on algorithmic variants (e.g., rumor-spreading rules) and time profiles, not the structural absence of a threshold or systemic risk machinery. The object differs (rumor propagation in social/communication networks vs. epidemic spreading in scale-free networks), but the methods do not cohere.
